text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: To live without hope is to cease to live.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: Courage is grace under pressure.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: The universe is change; life is opinion.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Love all, trust a few, do wrong to none.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Where there is no struggle, there is no strength.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: Life must be lived as play.
author: Plato

text: All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Without music, life would be a mistake.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: All the variety, all the charm, all the beauty of life is made up of light and shadow.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
author: Plato

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The purpose of life is a life of purpose.
author: Robert Byrne

text: It is our choices that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: J.K. Rowling

text: The mind is everything; what you think, you become.
author: Buddha

text: The only thing I know is that I know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: What we know is a drop, what we don't know is an ocean.
author: Isaac Newton

text: Wisdom begins in wonder.
author: Socrates

text: To love and be loved is to feel the sun from both sides.
author: David Viscott

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Judge a man by his questions rather than by his answers.
author: Voltaire

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Knowledge speaks, but wisdom listens.
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
author: Confucius

text: To be, or not to be, that is the question.
author: William Shakespeare

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: René Descartes

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
author: Confucius

text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: We are what we repeatedly do.
author: Aristotle

text: In the end, we will remember not the words of our enemies, but the silence of our friends.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: The only impossible journey is the one you never begin.
author: Tony Robbins

text: What is done cannot be undone.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: The mind unlearns with difficulty what it has long learned.
author: Seneca

text: Man is what he believes.
author: Anton Chekhov

text: Character is destiny.
author: Heraclitus

text: The only real mistake is the one from which we learn nothing.
author: Henry Ford

text: One cannot step twice in the same river.
author: Heraclitus

text: It is not length of life, but depth of life.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: All truly great thoughts are conceived while walking.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The greatest victory is over oneself.
author: Aristotle

text: Live your life as an exclamation rather than an explanation.
author: Isaac Newton

text: The purpose of art is washing the dust of daily life.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Beauty will save the world.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: That which does not kill us makes us stronger.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: A goal without a plan is just a wish.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: Courage is knowing what not to fear.
author: Plato

text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: Happiness is found in doing, not merely possessing.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: To love another person is to see the face of God.
author: Victor Hugo

text: It is not death that a man should fear.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Happiness is not an ideal of reason.
author: Immanuel Kant

text: The secret of life is to fall seven times.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: In the end, it's not the years in your life.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Love is composed of a single soul inhabiting two bodies.
author: Aristotle

text: The true sign of intelligence is not knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: To err is human, to forgive, divine.
author: Voltaire

text: Man is condemned to be free.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: All human actions have one or more causes.
author: Aristotle

text: Knowledge is power.
author: Francis Bacon

text: Happiness is the highest good.
author: Aristotle

text: He who opens a school door, closes a prison.
author: Victor Hugo

text: To be without some of the things you want is an indispensable part of happiness.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: Not life, but good life, is to be chiefly valued.
author: Socrates

text: The only thing that interferes with my learning is my education.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
